Center for Discovery accepts most commercial insurances. At the moment, Medicare and Medicaid are not accepted. For alternative resources, visit:  https://findtreatment.gov/ 

 

 

 

 

 
Frequently Asked Questions

 

 

How can I check if my insurance provider covers treatment?
 

Center for Discovery is pleased to be a preferred provider for most major insurance carriers. Check out our list of acceptable insurances. You can also contact your insurance provider or insurance customer support agent directly for further clarification on the costs covered.

What are my payment options if I’m not covered by insurance?
 

 Even if your insurance company is not on our in-network list, or you don’t have insurance at all, it is still possible to get eating disorder treatment. Center for Discovery accepts all major credit cards, eChecks and cash , as well as HSA/FSA debit cards. We provide complimentary benefit verification and can also work to obtain a single case agreement if necessary. Please call us today at 1-800-760-3934  to discuss your options for obtaining necessary treatment. 

I’m having trouble understanding what my insurance covers. What are my options?
 

 Check your insurance provider’s website or call them directly to clarify what your in-network and out-of-network benefits are for your plan. Each plan has a different deductible and out-of-pocket amount. Some plans don’t offer out-of-network benefits, in which case you would pay out-of-pocket for treatment services that are not in your insurance plan’s network. 

What if I have a PPO, EPO or HMO?
 

 Depending on your insurance provider, you will see it clearly marked on the front or back of your insurance card whether you have a preferred provider organization (PPO), exclusive provider organization (EPO) or health maintenance organization (HMO). You will have different levels of co-pays, premiums, deductibles and in-network and out-of-pocket costs depending on which type of insurance coverage you have. Feel free to reach out to us today for more information . 

Does insurance cover all of treatment?
 

 The level of eating disorder treatment coverage depends on your individual insurance plan specifications. Our team is happy to check which treatment services are covered and which (if any) might incur out-of-pocket costs. Simply fill out the brief insurance form and we will be in touch shortly . 

What time of year is best to get treatment?
 

There is no “right time” to get the treatment you need and deserve. But if you do have insurance coverage, using it before there are any changes to your current plan is ideal. For some, insurance plans can change before the beginning of a new year. Be sure to check when your current plan expires so you can utilize any benefits.

Do you offer telehealth treatment options?
 

 Yes, our online virtual programming is considered like our in-person programming, meaning that it can be covered by most major insurance companies. Online virtual treatment for those who need intensive outpatient (IOP) care is an ideal way to get effective support from the comfort of home. Telehealth is currently available in the following states: California, Connecticut, Florida, New Jersey, Texas and Washington. 

Does Center for Discovery take Medicaid insurance?
 

Center for Discovery accepts most commercial insurances. At the moment, Medicare and Medicaid are not accepted. For alternative resources, visit:  https://findtreatment.gov/ 

 

 

 

 
FIND HELP TODAY.

CALL 800.760.3934 

OR SCHEDULE A FREE CONSULTATION

rawMentions: Beacon Health Strategies
UNICARE – Beacon HS
Residential Treatment
Shawnee, KS (Residential)
Residential treatment centers (RTC)

Clients who received at least 7 residential treatment days (as weight was measured once per week), who had at least one day of weight restoration, and who had %TBW data present for admission and discharge were included in this analysis.
We provide treatment for only a small number of residential patients at one time to maintain an intimate setting and a high staff-to-resident ratio.

Treatment within a residential neighborhood or in an outpatient facility allows our patients to feel more comfortable in familiar surroundings and offers greater transferability of the healing experience to life after treatment.
Director of Nutrition & Wellness, Residential
VP of Operations Residential Division
Deandra has worked with individuals with eating disorders, self-injurious behaviors, mood disorders, and other co-occurring issues in both residential and outpatient treatment settings.
Costa worked as a therapist in a variety of settings throughout California, with experience in residential, partial hospitalization, intensive outpatient and private practice treatment programs.

Jamie (she/her/hers) is the Director of Nutrition and Wellness, for the residential level of care at Center for Discovery and has a private practice, where she utilizes a non-diet and dialectical behavior therapy (DBT) informed approach combining both nutrition and psychology.

She earned a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology from California State University, Dominguez Hills, and joined Center for Discovery in 2012 as a counselor at the residential eating disorder treatment program in La Habra, CA.
Abby has mentored and guided all Milieu Managers in the Eating Disorder Residential Facilities for the past ten years.

Marissa has worked in the acute/residential setting for eating disorder treatment since 2012 now becoming a Certified Eating Disorder Specialist and supervisor relentlessly pursuing higher education and professional development to provide quality care to her clients.
Marissa Joined the Discovery team in 2017 as the Program Director of the Orlando residential program, and quickly advanced to the Regional Director of Operations.
Marissa is a licensed professional counselor in Virginia, Georgia, and Florida overseeing the residential and outpatient locations in the MidAtlantic and Southeast region.
Treatment Program of Interest*: Please Choose One Adult Residential Treatment
Adolescent Residential Treatment

After fourteen years in the field of eating disorder treatment, Kacy is now the Director of Nutrition and Wellness, Outpatient, for Center for Discovery where she has developed the outpatient dietary program that has been implemented in over 28 facilities nationwide and has been part of the development and implementation of a diagnostic specific Binge Eating Disorder treatment program at Discovery.

rawMentions: Co-Occurring Disorders

With 25-plus years of experience in treating eating disorders and co-occurring conditions, our team understands how to create individualized recovery plans that are as unique as the people who trust us with their care.
At Center for Discovery, we have provided eating disorder and co-occurring disorder treatment for adolescents and adults of all genders for over 25 years.
Deandra has worked with individuals with eating disorders, self-injurious behaviors, mood disorders, and other co-occurring issues in both residential and outpatient treatment settings.
Costa’s areas of concentration have been in treating eating disorders, self-injurious behaviors, mood disorders, and other co-occurring issues in both adults and adolescents.
